input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Header>
        <RefreshView
            Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
            IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
        </RefreshView>
    </CollectionView.Header>

    <!-- Wrap the whole CollectionView in a RefreshView instead: -->
</CollectionView>
```

The standard pattern is to **wrap** the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing, Mode=TwoWay}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="›"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    // Fires on pull-to-refresh
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    // Fires on item tap
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelectedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(
            nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object>
            {
                ["Item"] = SelectedItem
            });

        SelectedItem = null;        // reset so re-tap works
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | OneWay | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` | **TwoWay** | VM reads the tap; resets to `null` after navigation |
| `IsRefreshing` | **TwoWay** | `RefreshView` sets it `true`; VM sets it `false` when done |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | OneWay | Triggers navigation logic |
| `RefreshView.Command` | OneWay | Triggers data reload |

**Three things people commonly miss:**
1. **`IsRefreshing` must be TwoWay** — you need to set it `false` in your VM or the spinner never stops.
2. **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigating — otherwise tapping the same item a second time won't fire the command.
3. **`RefreshView` wraps `CollectionView`**, not the other way around. Putting refresh logic inside the `CollectionView.Header` won't give you pull-to-refresh behavior.
